canonical_question: What are the transfer pricing rules for companies with international subsidiaries?
aliases: OECD transfer pricing methods CUP TNMM
constraints: OECD Guidelines are non-binding soft law; each country implements its own domestic TP rules
Pillar Two (15% global minimum tax) live in 40+ jurisdictions from Jan 2024
CbCR filing applies only to MNEs with consolidated revenue >= EUR 750 million
Functional analysis must be completed before selecting a TP method
TP rules interact with customs valuation, VAT/GST, thin capitalization, and CFC regimes

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User is asking about US-specific Section 482 rules and IRS penalties
User is asking about Pillar Two global minimum tax mechanics
User is asking about a specific bilateral tax treaty

related_to.label: GDPR compliance rules (parallel regulatory compliance domain)

inputs_needed.question: Does the company have related-party transactions across different tax jurisdictions?
options: Yes, goods and/or services between subsidiaries in different countries
Yes, intangibles (IP, trademarks, know-how) licensed cross-border
Yes, intercompany financing (loans, guarantees, cash pooling)

inputs_needed.question: What is the consolidated group revenue?
options: Below EUR 750 million — CbCR not required, but TP documentation still needed
EUR 750 million or above — full three-tiered documentation (master file, local file, CbCR)

inputs_needed.question: Is there a pending audit, proposed adjustment, or double taxation issue?
options: Yes — need to understand dispute resolution (MAP, APA, litigation)

applies_to.user_segment: Multinational enterprises with intercompany cross-border transactions
applies_to.context: Any company with related-party transactions across jurisdictions, including goods, services, intangibles, and financial transactions
